review_notes: The functions provided are generally well-aligned with the intent of controlling access and securing configurations in Kubernetes. However, the mapping of MP-2(1) to these functions requires a broader interpretation of 'media access' to include network and role-based access controls. The function names are consistent with the naming conventions and accurately describe the checks being performed.
title: Media Access - . ]
step1_reasoning: The control MP-2(1) focuses on media access, which can be interpreted in a Kubernetes context as controlling access to resources and ensuring secure configurations. This includes ensuring that no overly permissive roles are bound (k8s_rbac_no_cluster_admin_binding), restricting network access to and from pods (k8s_networkpolicy_default_deny_ingress and k8s_networkpolicy_default_deny_egress), and ensuring pods do not run with root privileges (k8s_pod_security_context_non_root). These checks collectively help in controlling access and securing the environment.
